codeReview.summary: Well-implemented core structured output types with comprehensive test coverage. The implementation follows AGENTS.md conventions, uses TDD pattern correctly, and includes excellent JSDoc documentation. All 21 tests pass covering all validation assertions. The worker correctly followed the backend-worker skill procedure, writing tests first then implementing. TypeScript strict typing is properly used with type inference from Zod schemas.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker handled zod-to-json-schema integration correctly. The library documentation pattern for converting Zod schemas is now captured in the code - zodToJsonSchema function uses $refStrategy: 'none' and target: 'jsonSchema7' options. This is a reasonable default that strips $schema and definitions from output.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/structured-output.ts:280-291 - zodToJsonSchema function documents the conversion options and cleanup of metadata

summary: Feature passes code review. Implementation quality is excellent with proper TypeScript types (discriminated unions, type guards, generics), comprehensive JSDoc documentation with examples, and 317 lines of tests covering all code paths. The worker went beyond the minimal feature description to also implement schema conversion utilities (zodToJsonSchema, convertToProviderSchema, stripUnsupportedFeatures) and validation functions (parseStructured, tryParseStructured) - this was appropriate as these are referenced in the validation contract (VAL-SCHEMA-002, VAL-SCHEMA-005, VAL-SCHEMA-007, etc.). No blocking issues found. Tests cover success paths, failure paths, malformed JSON handling, type narrowing, Google-specific schema transformations, nested objects, arrays, enums, and primitives.
